messageValues.message:
=You are a curious and inquisitive researcher conducting a survey with a user.
* In this conversation, you are only interested in one of the questions from the survey which you will ask.
* When asking an open-ended question, converse with the user to dig deeper into their answer and reveal insights into the user's experience. Do not ask other questions which do not try to expand the user's answer to the initial question.
* If this is closed-ended question, it is okay to receive a static response and move on to the next question.
* Only the initial question is numbered, following questions or messages should not be numbered or bulletpointed.
* If the user goes off-topic, doesn't want to answer the question or wants to do something else which is not related to the survey, then ignore what they say/ask and politely repeat the question.

=Either
* The user was asked a close-ended question and gave an adequate static response
* There are no further insights to be learned from the user's answer

=Either one of
* There are possibly more insights to be learned from the user's answer if we enquire. hint: did the user explaied or justified their answer?
* The user didn't answer the question

parameters.text:
={{ $('Set Variables1').first().json.title }}.
Welcome! Thank you for taking the time to participate in our survey.

You'll be asked a couple of pre-defined questions. For each question, we may ask follow-up questions to better understand your situation. If you want to skip to the next question at any time, simply reply with the "/next". Your responses will be recorded.

To start the survey, simply reply with "/next".

parameters.content:
## Try It Out
### This n8n template demonstrates how you can build a structured chat journey augmented with AI for your business.

In this scenario, a chatbot conducts a product satisfaction survey with a user using a predefined set of questions. For each question, the agent can dive deeper into the user's answers by asking follow-up questions.

This chatbot template isn't fully "agentic" which makes it a bit more complicated and scripted but ensures a more deterministic user journey; suitable when you need more guide-rails on the experience.

See example survey here: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

### How it works
* A chat session is started with the user who needs to enter the bot command "/next" to start the survey.
* Once started, the template pulls in questions from a google sheet to ask the user. Questions are asked in sequence from left to right.
* When the user answers the question, a text classifier node is used to determine 
if a follow-up question could be asked.
* If not, the survey proceeds to the next question. Otherwise, an AI Agent will generate a follow-up question based on the user's response.
* All answers and AI conversations are recorded in the Google Sheet.
* When all questions are answered, the template will stop the survey and give the user a chance to restart.

### How to use
* You'll need to setup a Telegram bot ([see docs]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
* Create a google sheet with an ID column. Populate the rest of the columns with your survey questions ([see sample]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
* Ensure you have a Redis instance to capture state. Either self-host or sign-up to [Upstash]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} for a free account.
* Update the "Set Variable" node with your google sheet ID and survey title.
* Share the bot with users to allow others to participate in your survey.

### Can I use this for WhatsApp?
Yes you can! Swapping out all telegram nodes for WhatsApp nodes should produce the same result.


### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

parameters.content:
## Try It Out
### This n8n template demonstrates how you can build a structured chat journey augmented with AI for your business.

In this scenario, a chatbot conducts a product satisfaction survey with a user using a predefined set of questions. For each question, the agent can dive deeper into the user's answers by asking follow-up questions.

This chatbot template isn't fully "agentic" which makes it a bit more complicated and scripted but ensures a more deterministic user journey; suitable when you need more guide-rails on the experience.

See example survey here: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

### How it works
* A chat session is started with the user who needs to enter the bot command "/next" to start the survey.
* Once started, the template pulls in questions from a google sheet to ask the user. Questions are asked in sequence from left to right.
* When the user answers the question, a text classifier node is used to determine 
if a follow-up question could be asked.
* If not, the survey proceeds to the next question. Otherwise, an AI Agent will generate a follow-up question based on the user's response.
* All answers and AI conversations are recorded in the Google Sheet.
* When all questions are answered, the template will stop the survey and give the user a chance to restart.

### How to use
* You'll need to setup a Telegram bot ([see docs]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
* Create a google sheet with an ID column. Populate the rest of the columns with your survey questions ([see sample]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
* Ensure you have a Redis instance to capture state. Either self-host or sign-up to [Upstash]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} for a free account.
* Update the "Set Variable" node with your google sheet ID and survey title.
* Share the bot with users to allow others to participate in your survey.

### Can I use this for WhatsApp?
Yes you can! Swapping out all telegram nodes for WhatsApp nodes should produce the same result.


### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Telegram Trigger".
Open the "Send Next Question" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send Response" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Has No Record?" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Is Survey Continue?" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get State2" node (n8n-nodes-base.redis) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Columns1" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Message Type1" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Bot Actions1" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Memory3" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Reset Agent Memory1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Memory4" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Start Session1" node (n8n-nodes-base.redis) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get State3" node (n8n-nodes-base.redis) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Increment Index1" node (n8n-nodes-base.redis) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Interview Agent1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Record1" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Update Answer2" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Last Bot Message1" node (n8n-nodes-base.redis) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Update Answer3" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Model2" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Model3" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Should Follow Up?1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Execution Data2" node (n8n-nodes-base.executionData) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create Record2" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Sticky Note8" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send Start" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send Start1" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Completed Survey" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create Record1" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note6" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note7" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set Variables1".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Get Command1".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Get Survey State1".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Append Responses1".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
